title: rosa arvensis var. repens
intro: Rosa arvensis var. repens, also known as creeping wild rose or downy rose, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant belonging to the rose family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rosa arvensis var. repens include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, flowers, and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes.
